reasoning: The current 10-year minus 3-month Treasury yield spread (T10Y3M) as of June 6, 2025 is 0.08. Over the last month, there has been a positive linear trend, with the spread reaching as high as 0.22 in late May before dipping into negative territory briefly in early June. The linear model from the FRED trend analysis estimates a 0.61 probability of the spread moving above 0.08 by mid-June (predicted value 0.12). Yield curve macro fundamentals also typically favor a positive spread during a rate-cutting cycle (which has been underway), so an upward bias is fundamentally justified, even though daily volatility is high. In accordance with the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase (probabilities move closer to 0.5) with longer forecast horizons, I am starting the first resolution date at the trend-implied 0.61 and degrading it steadily toward 0.52 for the December resolution date.
